report_name: Irish DPC Fines Health Service Executive €300,000 for GDPR Breaches in 2018 Hospital Attack

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: A ransomware attack in November 2018 targeted the Midlands Regional Hospital Tullamore's laboratory information system, encrypting the personal data of patients' diagnostic tests.

malware.name: Ransomware
malware.description: An unnamed ransomware was used to attack the Midlands Regional Hospital Tullamore in November 2018, encrypting patient diagnostic data.

identities.name: Health Service Executive (HSE)
identities.description: Ireland's national health service, which was fined €300,000 by the Data Protection Commission for GDPR infringements related to a 2018 ransomware attack on a hospital under its authority.

identities.name: Midlands Regional Hospital Tullamore
identities.description: The Irish hospital targeted by a ransomware attack in November 2018, which resulted in the encryption of diagnostic data for approximately 84,000 patients.

identities.name: Data Protection Commission
identities.description: Ireland's data protection authority that investigated the 2018 ransomware attack and imposed a €300,000 fine on the Health Service Executive for GDPR violations.
